title: Layer: Context
description: A slide introducing the Context layer. The pyramid is shown with the Context tier highlighted. Five sub-concepts are presented as distinct visual components: system prompts, Projects, CLAUDE.md, auto memory, and the /context command.

parts.title: Prompts
parts.description: A split layout: on the left, a raw chat interface showing a generic Claude response. On the right, the same interface with a system prompt injected — Claude now responds as a purpose-built internal tool with a defined persona and constrained output format. An arrow between the two sides is labeled 'System Prompt applied.'

parts.title: Projects
parts.description: A container diagram showing a Project as a box holding three components: Conversation History, CLAUDE.md, and Knowledge Files. An arrow from Knowledge Files points to a 'RAG' label, which then feeds into Claude's context window. The visual communicates that Claude always has the right context without manual re-pasting.

parts.title: CLAUDE.md
parts.description: Two stacked file cards side by side. Left card: project-scoped CLAUDE.md listing repo conventions, architecture decisions, and task defaults. Right card: user-scoped ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md listing global preferences — stack, tone, code review style, shortcuts. A monospace annotation shows the # edit shortcut for mid-session updates.

parts.title: Auto Memory — /memory
parts.description: A timeline or loop diagram showing Claude writing observations back to a memory file during a session, and that memory file being loaded at the start of the next session. A /memory command callout shows a sample list of stored preferences. The visual conveys a feedback loop that makes each session smarter.

parts.title: /context
parts.description: A terminal-style window displaying the output of /context — a structured list of what is currently loaded: files, memory entries, conversation turns, and the system prompt. Sections are color-coded. A caption reads: 'See exactly what Claude knows. Trim or add before a long task.'
